spec_match.rationale: Calls each deprecated getter (.calendars, .weekInfo, etc.) and its replacement method (.getCalendars(), .getWeekInfo(), etc.) on the same Intl.Locale instance — exactly the contrast the TC39 deprecation introduces.

interactive.rationale: Locale dropdown drives a live recomputation of every getter/method pair with side-by-side values and equality verdict.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'side-by-side: pick a locale, see what the deprecated .calendars-style getters return vs the new .getCalendars()-style methods … plus equality.' Page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles invalid locale tags, undefined returns, and equality via JSON.stringify (which is enough for primitive-array returns but fragile for object returns with insertion order changes). Doesn't show the deprecation console warning that Chrome 143 fires when the getter is used — that's the most useful signal for migrators.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5148228059398144 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only; .deprecated and .modern cell styling uses --accent-rose / --accent-emerald appropriately.

openQuestions.title: Surface the deprecation console warning
openQuestions.detail: Chrome 143 fires a deprecation warning when `.calendars` etc. is accessed. Catch this via the Reporting API (`document.body.addEventListener('reportingobserver')` after registering a ReportingObserver for 'deprecation') and surface the warning text in the page. That is the strongest signal for someone deciding when to migrate.

openQuestions.title: Add `timeFormats` to the matrix
openQuestions.detail: The TC39 proposal includes `timeFormats` / `getTimeFormats()` (used by the migration-helper sibling page) but this matrix omits it. Add the row so the matrix matches the full API surface.

openQuestions.title: Replace JSON.stringify equality with deep-equal
openQuestions.detail: Object key-order can flip between getter and method returns even when values are equivalent. A small deep-equal that ignores key order would stop false 'differ' verdicts.

summary: Clean side-by-side getter/method matrix. Biggest fix: capture and surface the deprecation console warning that Chrome 143 emits — that's the actual signal migrators need.
